# Japan Takaichi Cabinet Approval Rating – May 2026: Research Report

## Executive Summary

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, Japan's first female PM, leads the Second Takaichi Cabinet (formed 18 February 2026) following a historic LDP landslide in the February 2026 general election. Her cabinet continues to enjoy strong approval ratings in May 2026, though there has been a gradual decline from the extraordinary highs seen at the start of her premiership (75–83% in October–November 2025). As of mid-May 2026, approval ratings range from approximately **57–68%** depending on the polling firm, down from roughly 60–74% in early May and 62–72% in March 2026.

## Most Recent Polls (May 2026)

| Fieldwork Dates | Polling Firm | Approve (%) | Disapprove (%) | Und./NA (%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 15–18 May 2026 | **Jiji Press** | **59.4** | 19.7 | 20.9 |
| 16–17 May 2026 | **Asahi Shimbun** | **60** | 26 | 14 |
| 16–17 May 2026 | **Sankei/FNN** | **68** | 26.2 | 5.8 |
| 16–17 May 2026 | **Senkyo.com/JX** | **57.4** | 26.4 | 16.2 |
| 16–17 May 2026 | **Kyodo News** | **61.3** | 26.8 | 11.9 |
| 11–17 May 2026 | **Yoron Radar** | **57.8** | 35 | 7.2 |
| 8–10 May 2026 | **NHK** | **60.7** | 23.2 | 16.1 |
| 4–10 May 2026 | **Yoron Radar** | **57.6** | 34.1 | 8.3 |
| 2–3 May 2026 | **JNN (TBS)** | **74.2** | 24.3 | 1.5 |
| 27 Apr–3 May 2026 | **Yoron Radar** | **59.1** | 34.2 | 6.7 |

**Mid-May 2026 average (across major pollsters): approximately 60–62%**

Key notes on polling methodology variation:
- **Sankei/FNN** and **JNN** tend to produce the highest readings (68–74%)
- **Yoron Radar** and **Senkyo.com/JX** tend to produce the lowest readings (57–58%)
- The Jiji Press poll headline notes approval is "unchanged at 59.4%" with the top reason for support being "strong leadership"

## Approval Rating Trajectory

### Initial Highs (Oct–Nov 2025)
- **Sankei/FNN** (October 2025): **75.4%** (women aged 18-29: 91.7%)
- Various polls (late Oct–early Nov 2025): **65%–83%** — among the highest such ratings of any Japanese government in 20 years

### Post-Election Boost (February 2026)
- **Yomiuri/NNN** (18–19 Feb): **73%**
- **Sankei/FNN** (14–15 Feb): **72%**
- **Nikkei/TV Tokyo** (13–15 Feb): **69%**
- **NHK** (13–15 Feb): **65.2%**
- 52% of respondents said they wanted Takaichi to stay "as long as possible"

### March 2026 (Still Strong)
- **Nikkei/TV Tokyo** (27–29 Mar): **72%**
- **JNN** (28 Feb–1 Mar): **71.8%**
- **Yomiuri/NNN** (20–22 Mar): **71%**
- **Mainichi/SSRC** (28–29 Mar): **58%**

### April 2026 (Gradual Decline Begins)
- **Sankei/FNN** (18–19 Apr): **70.2%** (back above 70%)
- **Nikkei/TV Tokyo** (24–26 Apr): **69%** (down 3 points)
- **Yomiuri/NNN** (17–19 Apr): **66%**
- **Mainichi/SSRC** (18–19 Apr): **53%** — noted as "lowest since inception"
- **Yoron Radar** showed a **five consecutive weeks of decline** from 62.5% to 57.0% through April
- Key factor: **Strait of Hormuz crisis** casting a shadow

### May 2026 (Stabilization at Lower Level)
- Ratings appear to have stabilized in the high-50s to mid-60s range (57–68% depending on pollster)
- Jiji Press noted approval is "unchanged" at 59.4%
- Yoron Radar showed slight recovery from 57.0% → 59.1% → 57.6% → 57.8%

## Key Events Affecting Approval

### 1. 2025–2026 China–Japan Diplomatic Crisis
- **November 2025**: Takaichi stated that a Chinese attack on Taiwan could constitute an "existential crisis situation" for Japan, allowing military action in self-defence
- Chinese consul-general in Osaka, Xue Jian, made threatening comments on X, leading to a diplomatic row
- China imposed cultural boycotts, travel advisories, and cut seafood imports
- **January 2026**: China banned dual-use item exports to Japan for military use
- **Impact on approval**: Initially boosted Takaichi's popularity (rallying nationalist sentiment), but prolonged tensions created anxiety

### 2. 2026 General Election Landslide (8 February 2026)
- LDP won a historic **316 seats** — the most ever by a single party in Japan's postwar Lower House history
- LDP secured a two-thirds supermajority on its own
- Takaichi was especially popular among **young voters**: 84% of respondents in their 20s and 78% in their 30s backed her (vs. 67% overall)
- Opposition Centrist Reform Alliance (CDP-Komeito merger) collapsed to just 49 seats from 167

### 3. Strait of Hormuz Crisis (from late February 2026)
- **28 February 2026**: Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz following a US-Israeli air campaign against Iran
- Oil prices surpassed $100/barrel (8 March), peaking at $126/barrel — the largest disruption to world energy supply since the 1970s
- **Impact on Japan**: 74% of respondents expressed "anxiety" about gasoline/petroleum shortages (JNN, April 2026)
- **58%** expressed dissatisfaction with Takaichi's inflation/price measures (Sankei, May 2026)
- **70%** said energy conservation and frugality were "necessary" (Nikkei, April 2026)
- This is the primary factor driving the gradual approval decline in April–May 2026
- Yoron Radar headline: "Tug-of-war between diplomatic achievements and anxiety about daily life"

### 4. Economic Policy Issues
- Rising oil prices and inflation from the Hormuz crisis
- Debate over food consumption tax reduction — 42.5% support "even 1% if implemented quickly" (FNN, May 2026)
- Discussion of a "bachelor tax" (独身税) receiving mixed reviews
- Takaichi's ¥13.9 trillion ($92.19 billion) economic stimulus package
- Science, Technology and Innovation Basic Plan targeting ¥60 trillion in R&D spending by 2030

### 5. Dissatisfaction with Trump/US Relations
- Yoron Radar (April Week 2) noted "dissatisfaction with Trump" weighing on approval despite budget passage
- Energy crisis linked to US-Israeli military action against Iran

## Contextual Comparison
- Despite the decline, Takaichi's approval ratings remain **historically high** by Japanese standards
- Her ratings are significantly above the levels seen by her predecessor Shigeru Ishiba (who resigned due to poor election results)
- Even the "lowest since inception" Mainichi reading of 53% (April) would have been considered strong for most recent Japanese PMs
- The top reason for continued support cited is her "leadership" (リーダーシップ) according to Jiji Press

key_facts: Sanae Takaichi became Japan's first female Prime Minister on 21 October 2025, succeeding Shigeru Ishiba after winning the 2025 LDP presidential election.
The Second Takaichi Cabinet was formed on 18 February 2026 after the LDP won a historic landslide in the general election with 316 seats (two-thirds supermajority).
As of mid-May 2026, cabinet approval ratings range from approximately 57-68% across major pollsters, with a mid-point around 60-62%.
Approval has declined gradually from initial highs of 65-83% in October-November 2025, and post-election highs of 65-73% in February 2026.
The Strait of Hormuz crisis (from late February 2026, caused by US-Israeli air campaign against Iran) is the primary driver of declining approval, via rising oil/energy prices and inflation anxiety.
58% of respondents expressed dissatisfaction with Takaichi's inflation measures (Sankei/FNN, May 2026); 74% expressed anxiety about gasoline/petroleum shortages (JNN, April 2026).
Despite the decline, ratings remain historically high for a Japanese PM; the top reason for support is 'strong leadership' (Jiji Press, May 2026).
The 2025-2026 China-Japan diplomatic crisis (over Taiwan remarks) initially boosted Takaichi's popularity but prolonged tensions have contributed to public unease.
Takaichi is especially popular among young voters: 84% of those in their 20s and 78% in their 30s backed her cabinet at the time of the February 2026 election.
The LDP-Japan Innovation Party coalition replaced the long-standing LDP-Komeito coalition after Komeito broke from the LDP in October 2025.
Yoron Radar tracked five consecutive weeks of approval decline in April 2026 (62.5% → 57.0%), linked to the Hormuz crisis.
Approval appears to have stabilized in May 2026 — Jiji Press described it as 'unchanged' at 59.4%, and Yoron Radar showed slight fluctuation around 57-59%.
